library_overview.name: Alphalens
library_overview.description: Python library for performance analysis of predictive (alpha) stock factors
library_overview.current_status: Original repository archived; actively maintained forks available

integrations.pyfolio: Portfolio performance and risk analysis integration

repositories.citation: [1] Quantopian. "Alphalens: Performance analysis of predictive (alpha) stock factors." GitHub, Apache-2.0 License. https://github.com/quantopian/alphalens
[2] Stefan Jansen. "Alphalens-Reloaded: Performance analysis of predictive (alpha) stock factors." GitHub, 2025. https://github.com/stefan-jansen/alphalens-reloaded
[3] cloudQuant. "Alphalens: Enhanced performance analysis of predictive (alpha) factors." GitHub. https://github.com/cloudQuant/alphalens

alphalens.utils.description: Data preparation and utility functions for factor analysis

public_functions.name: get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns
public_functions.signature: get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns(factor, prices, groupby=None, binning_by_group=False, quantiles=5, bins=None, periods=(1, 5, 10), filter_zscore=20, groupby_labels=None, max_loss=0.35, zero_aware=False, cumulative_returns=True)
public_functions.description: Primary data pipeline function. Formats factor data, forward return data, and group mappings into a DataFrame suitable for Alphalens analysis.

factor.description: MultiIndex Series indexed by timestamp (level 0) and asset (level 1), containing alpha factor values

prices.description: DataFrame with date index and asset columns containing pricing data

groupby.description: Optional group assignments (e.g., sector information) for grouped analysis

binning_by_group.description: If True, perform separate quantile bucketing per group

quantiles.description: Integer for equal-sized quantiles or sequence of quantile thresholds

bins.description: Integer for equal-width bins or sequence of bin edges. Alternative to quantiles

periods.description: Tuple of forward return horizons in trading days

filter_zscore.description: Outlier filtering threshold in standard deviations

groupby_labels.description: Dictionary mapping group IDs to human-readable labels

max_loss.description: Maximum allowed data loss percentage (0.0 to 1.0). Raises MaxLossExceededError if exceeded

cumulative_returns.description: If True, compute cumulative forward returns

returns.description: MultiIndex DataFrame indexed by (date, asset) with columns: factor values, forward returns for each period, factor quantile/bin assignment, and optional group membership

raises: MaxLossExceededError: When data loss exceeds max_loss threshold

public_functions.name: get_clean_factor
public_functions.signature: get_clean_factor(factor, forward_returns, groupby=None, binning_by_group=False, quantiles=5, bins=None, groupby_labels=None, max_loss=0.35, zero_aware=False)
public_functions.description: Formats factor data when forward returns are already computed. Use this if you've pre-calculated forward returns.

returns.description: Same structure as get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns output

public_functions.name: compute_forward_returns
public_functions.description: Calculates N-period forward returns for each asset at each timestamp

returns.description: DataFrame with forward return columns for each specified period

public_functions.name: demean_forward_returns
public_functions.description: Converts returns to group-relative or universe-relative basis by subtracting mean returns

returns.description: DataFrame with normalized returns (same shape as input)

public_functions.name: quantize_factor
public_functions.description: Assigns factor values to quantile buckets

public_functions.name: rate_of_return
public_functions.description: Annualizes or standardizes returns to target frequency

returns.description: Annualized returns

public_functions.name: std_conversion
public_functions.description: Converts volatility to standardized period basis

returns.description: Standardized volatility

public_functions.name: timedelta_to_string
public_functions.description: Converts pd.Timedelta to string format (e.g., '1D', '5D')

public_functions.name: timedelta_strings_to_integers
public_functions.description: Converts list of timedelta strings to integers

public_functions.name: get_forward_returns_columns
public_functions.description: Identifies forward return columns in DataFrames

returns.description: List of column names that represent forward returns

public_functions.name: print_table
public_functions.description: Pretty-prints DataFrames/Series (HTML in Jupyter, text in console)

public_functions.name: infer_trading_calendar
public_functions.description: Detects trading calendar from data index frequency

public_functions.name: add_custom_calendar_timedelta
public_functions.description: Adds periods respecting custom trading calendars

public_functions.name: diff_custom_calendar_timedeltas
public_functions.description: Calculates period differences on custom calendars

public_functions.name: backshift_returns_series
public_functions.description: Shifts multi-indexed return series backward N observations

public_functions.name: rethrow
public_functions.description: Re-raises exceptions with supplementary context

public_functions.name: non_unique_bin_edges_error
public_functions.description: Decorator providing informative quantile binning errors

exceptions.name: MaxLossExceededError
exceptions.description: Raised when data loss exceeds the max_loss threshold during data preparation

exceptions.name: NonMatchingTimezoneError
exceptions.description: Raised when timezone mismatches are detected between factor and price data

alphalens.tears.description: Tear sheet generation module providing comprehensive factor analysis visualizations and reports

classes.name: GridFigure
classes.description: Helper class for grid-based plotting operations

methods.name: next_cell
methods.description: Advances to the next cell in the grid

methods.name: next_row
methods.description: Advances to the next row in the grid

public_functions.name: create_full_tear_sheet
public_functions.description: Creates a comprehensive tear sheet combining returns analysis, information coefficient analysis, and turnover analysis. This is the primary function for complete factor evaluation.

factor_data.description: Output from get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns. MultiIndex DataFrame indexed by (date, asset) with columns: factor values, forward returns, quantiles, and optional groups

long_short.description: If True, compute long-short portfolio by demeaning returns and factor values across the universe

group_neutral.description: If True, perform group-level demeaning. Each group weights equally in cumulative returns

by_group.description: If True, display separate graphs for each group

returns.description: Displays comprehensive visualizations including returns by quantile, IC analysis, turnover metrics, and summary statistics

public_functions.name: create_returns_tear_sheet
public_functions.description: Creates a tear sheet focused on returns analysis across factor quantiles. Shows mean returns by quantile, cumulative returns, and return distributions.

returns.description: Displays returns-focused visualizations

public_functions.name: create_information_tear_sheet
public_functions.description: Creates a tear sheet evaluating factor predictiveness through Information Coefficient (IC) analysis. Shows IC time series, distributions, and group breakdowns.

returns.description: Displays IC-focused visualizations including time series, histograms, Q-Q plots, and heatmaps

public_functions.name: create_turnover_tear_sheet
public_functions.description: Creates a tear sheet examining portfolio rebalancing frequency and position changes. Analyzes factor stability through rank autocorrelation and quantile turnover.

turnover_periods.description: Custom periods for turnover analysis. If None, uses periods from factor_data

returns.description: Displays turnover metrics and factor autocorrelation plots

public_functions.name: create_summary_tear_sheet
public_functions.description: Creates a condensed summary tear sheet with key metrics from returns, information, and turnover analysis.

returns.description: Displays compact summary with key performance indicators

public_functions.name: create_event_returns_tear_sheet
public_functions.signature: create_event_returns_tear_sheet(factor_data, returns, avgretplot=(5, 15), long_short=True, group_neutral=False, std_bar=True, by_group=False)
public_functions.description: Creates a tear sheet displaying average cumulative returns within event windows (before/after event dates).

returns.description: Returns data for event analysis

avgretplot.description: Tuple of (periods_before, periods_after) for averaging window

std_bar.description: If True, display standard error bars

public_functions.name: create_event_study_tear_sheet
public_functions.description: Creates a tear sheet for analyzing specific events with optional event-window return visualization.

returns.description: Returns data

rate_of_ret.description: If True, compute rate of return

n_bars.description: Number of bars for event distribution histogram

alphalens.performance.description: Computational functions for factor analytics and portfolio metrics. Handles all quantitative calculations for factor evaluation.

public_functions.name: factor_information_coefficient
public_functions.description: Computes the Spearman Rank Correlation based Information Coefficient (IC) between factor values and N-period forward returns for each period. IC measures the predictive power of the factor.

group_adjust.description: If True, demean forward returns by group before computing IC

by_group.description: If True, compute period-wise IC separately for each group

returns.description: DataFrame with Spearman rank correlation coefficients. Index: dates, Columns: forward return periods

public_functions.interpretation: IC > 0.03 with IR > 0.5 generally indicates strong predictive ability. IC measures monotonic relationship between factor ranks and future returns.

public_functions.name: mean_information_coefficient
public_functions.description: Computes mean IC grouped by time periods or groups. Answers: 'What is the mean IC for each month?' or 'What is the mean IC for each group?'

by_time.description: Time grouping period (e.g., 'M' for monthly, 'W' for weekly)

returns.description: DataFrame of aggregated IC statistics grouped by specified dimension

public_functions.name: mean_return_by_quantile
public_functions.description: Computes mean returns for factor quantiles across forward return periods. Core function for evaluating if higher factor values lead to higher returns.

by_date.description: If True, compute returns separately for each date

by_group.description: If True, compute returns separately for each group

demeaned.description: If True, demean returns across universe

group_adjust.description: If True, demean returns by group

returns.description: (mean_returns_df, std_error_df) - Two DataFrames with mean returns and standard errors by quantile

public_functions.name: compute_mean_returns_spread
public_functions.description: Computes the difference between mean returns of two quantiles (typically top vs bottom for long-short analysis). Optionally includes standard error.

returns.description: (spread_series, std_error_series or None) - Long-short return spread and optional standard error

public_functions.name: factor_returns
public_functions.description: Computes period-wise returns for portfolio weighted by factor values. Generates the actual returns time series for a factor-based strategy.

demeaned.description: If True, demean factor values (creates long-short portfolio)

group_adjust.description: If True, demean within groups (group-neutral portfolio)

equal_weight.description: If True, use equal weights instead of factor-value weights

by_asset.description: If True, return per-asset returns instead of portfolio returns

returns.description: DataFrame of period-wise portfolio returns, one column per forward return period

public_functions.name: factor_weights
public_functions.description: Computes asset weights by normalizing factor values. Divides by sum of absolute values to achieve gross leverage of 1.

returns.description: Series with normalized asset weights (sums to 1 in absolute value)

public_functions.name: factor_alpha_beta
public_functions.description: Computes alpha (excess returns), alpha t-stat (significance), and beta (market exposure) through regression analysis.

returns.description: Benchmark returns for regression. If None, uses equal-weight portfolio
Series containing ['alpha', 'beta', 't-stat'] - Regression-based performance attribution

public_functions.name: factor_rank_autocorrelation
public_functions.description: Computes autocorrelation of mean factor ranks across time periods. Measures factor stability and predicts turnover. Low autocorrelation implies high turnover.

period.description: Number of periods for autocorrelation lag

returns.description: Series of rolling autocorrelations indexed by date

public_functions.interpretation: High autocorrelation (>0.7) indicates stable factor rankings. Low autocorrelation (<0.3) indicates high turnover and transaction costs.

public_functions.name: quantile_turnover
public_functions.description: Computes the proportion of names in a factor quantile that were not in that quantile in the previous period. Measures portfolio rebalancing frequency.

quantile.description: Quantile number to analyze (e.g., 1 for bottom, 5 for top)

returns.description: Series of turnover percentages (0.0 to 1.0) indexed by date

public_functions.interpretation: Turnover of 0.5 means 50% of positions changed. High turnover increases transaction costs.

public_functions.name: cumulative_returns
public_functions.description: Computes cumulative returns from simple daily returns. Converts period returns into growth trajectory.

returns.description: Series of period returns
Cumulative return series (growth of $1)

public_functions.name: factor_cumulative_returns
public_functions.signature: factor_cumulative_returns(factor_data, period, long_short=True, group_neutral=False, equal_weight=False, quantiles=None, groups=None)
public_functions.description: Simulates portfolio using the factor and returns cumulative returns over time. Complete backtest simulation.

period.description: Forward return period to use for simulation (e.g., '1D', '5D')

quantiles.description: Specific quantiles to include (None = all quantiles)

groups.description: Specific groups to include (None = all groups)

returns.description: Cumulative return series indexed by date

public_functions.name: factor_positions
public_functions.signature: factor_positions(factor_data, period, long_short=True, group_neutral=False, equal_weight=False, quantiles=None, groups=None)
public_functions.description: Simulates portfolio and returns asset positions as percentage of total portfolio. Shows allocation evolution over time.

returns.description: DataFrame with datetime index and asset columns showing position percentages

public_functions.name: positions
public_functions.description: Builds net position values time series showing portfolio percentage invested in each position.

returns.description: Position time series

public_functions.name: average_cumulative_return_by_quantile
public_functions.signature: average_cumulative_return_by_quantile(factor_data, returns, periods_before=10, periods_after=15, demeaned=True, group_adjust=False, by_group=False)
public_functions.description: Computes average cumulative returns by quantile in event windows. Used for event study analysis.

returns.description: Returns data for event analysis

periods_before.description: Number of periods before event to include

periods_after.description: Number of periods after event to include

returns.description: DataFrame with mean/std returns indexed by quantile and event time period

public_functions.name: common_start_returns
public_functions.signature: common_start_returns(factor, returns, before, after, cumulative=False, mean_by_date=False, demean_by=None)
public_functions.description: Extracts event-aligned return windows. For each (date, asset) in factor, builds a return series from 'before' to 'after' periods.

returns.description: Returns data

mean_by_date.description: If True, average across events on same date

returns.description: DataFrame with aligned return series (index: -before to after)

public_functions.name: create_pyfolio_input
public_functions.signature: create_pyfolio_input(factor_data, period, capital=None, long_short=True, group_neutral=False, equal_weight=False, quantiles=None, groups=None, benchmark_period='1D')
public_functions.description: Simulates portfolio and formats output for Pyfolio integration. Provides returns, positions, and benchmark series.

returns.description: (returns_series, positions_df, benchmark_series) - Formatted for Pyfolio analysis

alphalens.plotting.description: Visualization functions for factor analysis. All plotting functions return matplotlib Axes objects and can be customized.

decorators.name: customize
decorators.description: Decorator that applies consistent plotting context and axis styling to plot functions

context_functions.name: axes_style
context_functions.description: Creates Alphalens default axes style context, calling seaborn.axes_style() with custom settings

context_functions.name: plotting_context
context_functions.description: Creates Alphalens default plotting context with larger fonts for readability

public_functions.name: plot_ic_ts
public_functions.description: Plots Information Coefficient time series with rolling mean overlay. Shows IC evolution and moving average for trend identification.

ic.description: IC data from factor_information_coefficient(), indexed by date with IC values per forward return period

ax.description: Axes to plot on. If None, creates new figure

returns.description: The axes that were plotted on

public_functions.name: plot_ic_hist
public_functions.description: Plots histogram of Information Coefficient distribution. Useful for assessing IC consistency and normality.

public_functions.name: plot_ic_qq
public_functions.description: Plots Q-Q plot of IC against theoretical distribution. Tests if IC follows expected distribution (typically normal).

public_functions.name: plot_ic_by_group
public_functions.description: Plots IC comparison across groups (e.g., sectors). Shows which groups the factor predicts best.

public_functions.name: plot_monthly_ic_heatmap
public_functions.description: Plots heatmap of monthly IC values. Reveals seasonal patterns and time-varying factor effectiveness.

mean_monthly_ic.description: Mean IC by month (from mean_information_coefficient with by_time='M')

public_functions.name: plot_quantile_returns_bar
public_functions.description: Plots mean returns by factor quantile as bar chart. Core visualization for evaluating monotonic return relationship.

mean_ret_by_q.description: Mean returns by quantile from mean_return_by_quantile()

by_group.description: If True, show separate bars by group

ylim_percentiles.description: Tuple of (lower, upper) percentiles for y-axis limits

public_functions.interpretation: Expect monotonic increase from Q1 to Q5 for predictive factors. Spread between Q1 and Q5 indicates long-short profitability.

public_functions.name: plot_quantile_returns_violin
public_functions.description: Plots return distribution by quantile using violin plots. Shows full distribution, not just means.

public_functions.name: plot_cumulative_returns
public_functions.description: Plots cumulative returns of factor-based strategy. Shows growth of $1 over time.

public_functions.name: plot_cumulative_returns_by_quantile
public_functions.description: Plots cumulative returns separately for each quantile. Visualizes performance spread across factor rankings.

public_functions.name: plot_quantile_average_cumulative_return
public_functions.description: Plots average cumulative returns in event windows. Used for event study analysis showing returns before/after events.

by_quantile.description: If True, plot separate lines per quantile

std_bar.description: If True, show standard error bars

public_functions.name: plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series
public_functions.description: Plots long-short return spread (top quantile - bottom quantile) over time with confidence bands. Key metric for strategy profitability.

bandwidth.description: Number of standard errors for confidence band width

public_functions.name: plot_factor_rank_auto_correlation
public_functions.description: Plots factor rank autocorrelation over time. Indicates factor stability and turnover expectations.

public_functions.name: plot_top_bottom_quantile_turnover
public_functions.description: Plots turnover for top and bottom quantiles. Critical for estimating transaction costs in long-short strategies.
public_functions.interpretation: High turnover (>50%) indicates frequent rebalancing and high transaction costs. Aim for <30% for practical implementation.

public_functions.name: plot_events_distribution
public_functions.description: Plots histogram of event timing distribution. Shows when events occur throughout the analysis period.

public_functions.name: plot_information_table
public_functions.description: Displays tabular summary of IC statistics including mean, std, IR (Information Ratio).

returns.description: Displays formatted table

public_functions.name: plot_returns_table
public_functions.description: Displays formatted table of return metrics including alpha, beta, and quantile returns.

returns.description: Displays formatted table

public_functions.name: plot_quantile_statistics_table
public_functions.description: Displays table of descriptive statistics for each quantile (count, mean, std, etc.).

returns.description: Displays formatted table

public_functions.name: plot_turnover_table
public_functions.description: Displays table of turnover and stability metrics.

returns.description: Displays formatted table

factor_analysis.definition: Systematic evaluation of whether a quantitative signal (factor) has predictive power for future asset returns
factor_analysis.purpose: Determine if a factor can be profitably traded before implementing in production
workflow: 1. Define factor (signal/alpha) as time series of asset rankings
3. Analyze returns, IC, turnover via tear sheets
4. Evaluate if factor meets profitability and implementability criteria

quantiles.definition: Factor values are ranked and divided into equal-sized groups (typically 5 or 10 quantiles)
quantiles.purpose: Convert continuous factor values into discrete buckets for portfolio construction and analysis

interpretation.monotonic_relationship: Ideal: Q5 returns > Q4 > Q3 > Q2 > Q1 (higher factor = higher returns)

quantiles.long_short_strategy: Long top quantile (Q5), short bottom quantile (Q1), profit from spread

binning_options.quantiles: Equal-sized buckets (each has ~same number of assets)
binning_options.bins: Equal-width buckets (each covers same factor value range)
binning_options.binning_by_group: Separate quantile ranking within each group (e.g., per sector)

information_coefficient.name: Information Coefficient (IC)
information_coefficient.definition: Spearman rank correlation between factor values and subsequent forward returns

interpretation.positive_ic: Higher factor values predict higher returns (good for long strategies)
interpretation.negative_ic: Higher factor values predict lower returns (good for short strategies)

information_ratio.name: IR
information_ratio.interpretation: Sharpe ratio equivalent for IC. IR > 0.5 considered good, IR > 1.0 excellent
information_ratio.purpose: Measures consistency of predictive power, accounting for IC volatility

time_series_analysis.ic_ts_plot: Shows IC over time to identify regime changes
time_series_analysis.monthly_heatmap: Reveals seasonal patterns in factor effectiveness

advantages: Robust to outliers (uses ranks, not raw values)
Comparable across different factors and markets
Direct measure of monotonic predictive relationship

quantile_returns.definition: Mean forward returns for assets in each factor quantile
quantile_returns.calculation: For each quantile, compute average returns of all assets in that bucket

key_metrics.mean_return_by_quantile: Average return for Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, Q5
key_metrics.monotonicity: Whether returns increase consistently from Q1 to Q5

quantile_returns.cumulative_returns: Growth of $1 invested using factor strategy over time

turnover.definition: Frequency of portfolio position changes due to factor reranking
turnover.importance: High turnover leads to high transaction costs, reducing net profitability

quantile_turnover.definition: Proportion of assets in a quantile that were not in that quantile in previous period

factor_rank_autocorrelation.definition: Correlation between current factor ranks and previous period ranks

practical_considerations: Factors with IC=0.05 but turnover=0.8 may be unprofitable after costs
Factors with IC=0.03 and turnover=0.2 may be highly profitable
Consider holding period vs rebalancing frequency trade-off

grouped_analysis.definition: Breaking down factor performance by categories (typically sectors/industries)
purpose: Identify which groups the factor predicts best

group_neutral_portfolios.construction: Within each group, long top-ranked assets and short bottom-ranked
group_neutral_portfolios.benefits: Removes group (sector) exposure, isolates factor effect
group_neutral_portfolios.use_case: When you want factor returns independent of sector performance

analysis_outputs.ic_by_group: Shows which sectors have strongest predictive power

grouped_analysis.groupby_parameter: Pass sector/industry mapping as Series or dict to utils functions

forward_returns.definition: Returns measured from factor observation date to N periods in the future
forward_returns.purpose: Align factor values with subsequent returns to measure predictive power
forward_returns.multiple_periods: Analyze same factor across different holding periods to find optimal timeframe
forward_returns.lookback_bias_prevention: Alphalens ensures factor at time t only uses returns from t+1 onward

prices_input.requirement: Must extend beyond last factor date by max(periods) to compute forward returns

factor_data_output.description: Standard format consumed by all tears and performance functions

data_structure.max_loss_parameter: Controls acceptable data loss during alignment. Default 0.35 means up to 35% of factor data can be dropped due to missing prices or NaNs

parameters_to_consider: max_loss: 0.35 default, lower if data quality is critical

step_2_quick_analysis.decision: If promising, proceed to full analysis

step_5_pyfolio_integration.purpose: Risk analysis, drawdowns, factor exposures via Pyfolio

predictive_power.check: IC should be consistent over time (stable mean, low variance)

profitability.check: Spread should exceed transaction costs after adjusting for turnover

monotonicity.metric: Return progression Q1 < Q2 < Q3 < Q4 < Q5
monotonicity.acceptable: Q5 >> Q1 even if middle quantiles not perfectly ordered

group_robustness.check: Factor works across multiple groups, not just one sector
group_robustness.risk: Factor concentrated in one sector = sector bet, not true alpha

time_consistency.check: IC and returns relatively stable across different time periods
time_consistency.risk: Factor effective only in specific market regimes may not generalize

common_patterns: Most production factors use 5 quantiles for simplicity
1D, 5D, 21D periods are common for intraday, weekly, monthly strategies
Group-neutral construction common in institutional quant funds to control sector exposure
IC analysis preferred over raw return analysis as it's robust to outliers
Turnover analysis often reveals unprofitable factors with good IC
best_practices: Always run get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns first to validate data alignment
Check max_loss warnings - high data loss indicates data quality issues
Use create_summary_tear_sheet for quick factor screening
Compare multiple holding periods to find optimal trade frequency
Examine both IC and returns - high IC with low returns may indicate scale/capacity issues
Account for transaction costs using turnover metrics before declaring profitability
Use group_neutral=True to remove market/sector beta and isolate alpha
Look for IC>0.03 as minimum threshold for practical factors
Monitor IC consistency - volatile IC suggests regime-dependent factor
Cross-reference autocorrelation with turnover to predict implementation costs
pitfalls: Ignoring turnover - factor may be unprofitable after transaction costs
Not providing enough price history - need prices extending past last factor date by max(periods)
Timezone mismatches between factor and price data
Using too many quantiles with small universes (need sufficient assets per quantile)
Mistaking high IC for profitability - must check return magnitudes
Overfitting to specific time period - factor may not generalize
Not accounting for survivorship bias in data
Assuming factors work equally across all market conditions
Neglecting capacity constraints - IC-based metrics don't capture market impact
emerging_trends: Integration with modern ML pipelines (factor feature engineering)
Real-time factor monitoring vs batch analysis
Alternative data factors (sentiment, satellite, web traffic)
Factor decay analysis (how quickly IC degrades)

implementation_recommendations.rationale: Fast, covers key metrics, identifies obviously bad factors quickly

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use create_full_tear_sheet() with group_neutral=True, analyze turnover carefully
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Comprehensive analysis, accounts for sector effects, validates implementability

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use create_pyfolio_input() to export to Pyfolio for risk analysis
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Pyfolio provides deeper risk metrics, drawdown analysis, factor exposures

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use binning_by_group=True with by_group=True in tear sheets
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Enables within-sector ranking and separate analysis per sector

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use periods=(1,) for 1-day returns, monitor turnover closely
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Short holding periods increase turnover - must verify profitability after costs
Longer periods reduce turnover, better for low-frequency strategies

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use create_event_study_tear_sheet() with custom event windows
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Specialized for analyzing returns around specific events (earnings, M&A, etc.)
Removes extreme outliers that may distort analysis
Ensures sufficient assets per quantile for meaningful statistics
More quantiles provide better resolution with sufficient sample size

popular_solutions: Alphalens-reloaded is becoming the preferred fork due to active maintenance
Integration with Zipline (backtesting) and Pyfolio (risk analysis) is standard workflow
Jupyter notebooks are primary environment for factor research with Alphalens
Factor combination strategies often use Alphalens to evaluate individual factors before ensemble
controversial_topics: Whether IC is sufficient vs needing to analyze actual returns
Appropriate IC threshold for production factors (0.03 vs 0.05)
Value of group-neutral construction vs accepting sector bets
Transaction cost modeling accuracy using turnover metrics
expert_opinions: Stefan Jansen (maintainer of alphalens-reloaded): Active maintenance critical for modern Python compatibility
Quantopian community: IC>0.05 with IR>0.5 is 'really good', IC>0.03 is minimum viable
Practitioners emphasize turnover analysis often more important than IC for profitability
Combining multiple weak factors (IC 0.02-0.03) can yield stronger ensemble factor

documentation_sources.title: Alphalens-Reloaded Documentation
documentation_sources.description: Updated documentation for actively maintained fork
